skills.name: skill-creator
skills.short_description: Create new skills, optimize triggering, run evals
skills.description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.

skills.name: frontend-design
skills.short_description: Distinctive, production-grade UI that avoids AI slop
skills.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

mcp_servers.name: weather-mcp
mcp_servers.description: Global weather MCP server — current conditions and N-day forecast for any city, postcode, or lat/lng worldwide. Powered by Open-Meteo (free, no API key required for normal use). Replaces the US-only NWS-based servers.
mcp_servers.post_install_message: Requires `uv` (one-time: `pip install uv` or `brew install uv`). First invocation downloads the package and dependencies into an isolated env automatically — no separate pip install needed.

plugins.name: productivity
plugins.short_description: Task management, workplace memory, visual dashboard
plugins.description: Manage tasks (TASKS.md), build up workplace memory (people, projects, terminology, shorthand), and operate from a visual dashboard. Includes /start to bootstrap, /update to sync from connectors, and a memory-management skill that decodes nicknames/acronyms/codenames so the agent acts like a colleague. Forked from anthropics/knowledge-work-plugins with empty .mcp.json — install the connectors you actually use via /mcp install.

subagents.name: trip-planner
subagents.short_description: Day-by-day trip itinerary, food, and budget tips
subagents.description: Plans a trip end-to-end from a destination, dates/length, and budget level: a concrete day-by-day itinerary (morning/afternoon/evening with named places + rainy-day backups), where to eat with price bands, budget tips, and watch-outs. Uses WebSearch to sanity-check hours and prices when available. Spawn via Task(agent: "trip-planner") or /agent trip-planner.

subagents.name: meal-planner
subagents.short_description: A week of meals + grocery list from your diet & budget
subagents.description: Builds a 7-day meal plan and an aisle-grouped grocery list from your household size, dietary needs, budget, and cook-time. Reads a pantry/fridge list if you point it at one and plans to use up what you already have first to cut waste. Spawn via Task(agent: "meal-planner") or /agent meal-planner.

subagents.name: research-researcher
subagents.short_description: Topic → distinct search angles with concrete query phrases
subagents.description: Turns a research topic into a set of distinct search angles, each with concrete, diverse query phrases — the planning step that makes web research thorough instead of one-shot. Avoids angles you mark as already covered. Read-only. Spawn via Task(agent: "research-researcher") or /agent research-researcher.

subagents.name: research-synthesizer
subagents.description: Turns a set of sources into one coherent write-up where every substantive claim maps to a numbered [N] source — the synthesis step of a research pipeline. Reads the sources, cites only what they support, and refuses fabricated citations. Spawn via Task(agent: "research-synthesizer") or /agent research-synthesizer.

subagents.name: research-reviewer
subagents.short_description: Adversarial fact-check of a cited write-up
subagents.description: Adversarial peer-reviewer for a cited write-up: checks each [N] claim against its source, flags overstatements, gaps, and contradictions, and returns a structured ship/revise/rework verdict with blocking issues. Read-only. Spawn via Task(agent: "research-reviewer") or /agent research-reviewer.

subagents.name: research-summarizer
subagents.short_description: Several related docs → one one-page briefing
subagents.description: Condenses several related documents or research pages into a single one-page briefing: key findings with source attribution, where the sources agree and disagree, and the open threads. Synthesises only what's in the inputs. Read-only. Spawn via Task(agent: "research-summarizer") or /agent research-summarizer.

subagents.name: fact-checker
subagents.short_description: Verify each claim in a doc against its cited source
subagents.description: Verifies every factual claim in a document against its cited source — re-reads the source and rules verified/contested/opinion/unsupported, matching numbers and dates strictly. Read-only; never edits the doc or invents a source. Spawn via Task(agent: "fact-checker") or /agent fact-checker.

subagents.name: transcriber
subagents.short_description: Recording or messy transcript → clean speaker-attributed text
subagents.description: Turns a meeting/interview recording (via any available transcription tool) or a raw transcript into clean, speaker-attributed, lightly-timestamped text — drops filler, fixes run-ons, preserves every word's meaning. Doesn't summarize. Spawn via Task(agent: "transcriber") or /agent transcriber.

subagents.name: outliner
subagents.description: Turns an idea + audience + source material into a structured outline (parts → sections → beats) with a per-section premise, rough size, and source references — the cheap planning gate before drafting. Read-only. Spawn via Task(agent: "outliner") or /agent outliner.

subagents.name: classifier
subagents.short_description: Batch-sort items into labels with rationale + confidence
subagents.description: Sorts a batch of items (emails, tickets, messages, docs) into a caller-supplied label set, each with a one-line rationale and confidence, and escalates high-stakes/ambiguous items for a human instead of guessing. Read-only. Spawn via Task(agent: "classifier") or /agent classifier.
